subject: AP Macroeconomics
classification_standard: A student who has completed ONLY this unit should be able to answer this question.

units.name: Basic Economic Concepts
distinguishing_features: No international finance (only basic trade concepts)
example_questions: Calculate opportunity cost from PPF
Determine comparative advantage from output data
Find terms of trade range
PPF outward shift from technology (basic concept, not growth theory)

units.name: Economic Indicators and the Business Cycle
core_concepts: Types of unemployment (frictional, structural, cyclical, seasonal)
distinguishing_features: No market models (AD-AS, money market, loanable funds)
Natural rate of unemployment is a DEFINITION (U2), not a long-run equilibrium concept (U5)
example_questions: Calculate GDP using expenditure approach
Identify type of unemployment
Calculate inflation rate from CPI
Identify business cycle stage

units.name: National Income and Price Determination
core_concepts: Components of AD (C, I, G, NX)
Long-Run Aggregate Supply (LRAS) in AD-AS context
Changes in AD-AS in short run
excluded_concepts: Central bank actions (OMO, discount rate, reserve requirements)
Long-run economic growth (production function, technology, human capital)
distinguishing_features: Fiscal policy ONLY (no monetary policy tools)
boundary_rules: Monetary terms (central bank, money supply) in WRONG OPTIONS only → still U3 (distractors)
Monetary terms in CORRECT ANSWER or question stem → U4 or U5 (core concept)
Fiscal policy + interest rate effect in AD-AS context → U3 (monetary is side effect)
Fiscal policy + interest rate in loanable funds context → U4 (loanable funds market)
SRPC = U3 (short-run Phillips curve taught in U3)
LRPC = U5 (long-run Phillips curve taught in U5)
LRAS in AD-AS equilibrium = U3 (taught as part of AD-AS model)
LRAS shifting outward (growth) = U5 (economic growth context)
example_questions: AD shifts right from increased consumer confidence → U3
Government spending multiplier calculation → U3
SRPC shows trade-off between unemployment and inflation → U3
Stagflation cause and effect → U3
Automatic stabilizer during recession → U3

units.name: Financial Sector
excluded_concepts: Fiscal policy (government spending, taxes as main topic)
AD-AS equilibrium analysis (unless testing monetary transmission)
Exchange rates (unless domestic interest rate effect)
distinguishing_features: Money and banking are THE focus
Interest rate determination (money market, loanable funds)
boundary_rules: Monetary policy affecting AD → U4 (monetary transmission mechanism)
Fiscal policy in background + loanable funds foreground → U4
Money demand/supply = U4 (not taught in U3)
Budget deficit raising interest rates in loanable funds → U4
Budget deficit raising interest rates in AD-AS context → U3 (side effect of fiscal policy)
example_questions: Money demand increases, what happens to interest rate? → U4
Fed sells bonds, effect on money supply → U4
Money multiplier calculation → U4
Loanable funds market equilibrium → U4
Monetary policy transmission to AD → U4

units.name: Long-Run Consequences of Stabilization Policies
excluded_concepts: AD-AS model mechanics (shifts, equilibrium) as standalone
Monetary policy tools alone (OMO, discount rate)
distinguishing_features: Growth theory (production function, human capital, technology)
boundary_rules: EXPLICIT combination of fiscal + monetary actions → U5 (e.g., 'govt spending increases while central bank sells bonds')
Long-run growth (PPF outward, LRAS shifting from productivity) → U5
Crowding out → U5 (explicitly taught in U5)
example_questions: Which fiscal-monetary combo corrects recession without inflation? → U5
Human capital investment shifts production function → U5
Crowding out from large budget deficit → U5
LRPC is vertical at natural rate → U5
Policy mix to reduce inflation without recession → U5

units.name: Open Economy-International Trade and Finance
excluded_concepts: Domestic AD-AS (unless exchange rate effect on AD)
Domestic fiscal policy (unless through trade channel)
Domestic monetary policy (unless through exchange rate)
example_questions: Exchange rate appreciation effect on net exports → U6
BOP account classification → U6
Tariff effect on domestic price and quantity → U6
Capital inflow effect on loanable funds → U6 (or U4 if domestic interest rate focus)

cross_unit_rules.situation: Fiscal policy + monetary terms in options
cross_unit_rules.rule: If monetary terms are ONLY in wrong options (distractors) → U3. If monetary terms are in the correct answer or central to the question → U4 or U5.
If testing AD shift FROM monetary policy (transmission mechanism) → U4. If testing AD-AS equilibrium WITH monetary policy as given → U3 or U4 depending on context.

cross_unit_rules.situation: Interest rate changes from fiscal policy
cross_unit_rules.rule: If loanable funds market shown → U4. If AD-AS graph shown → U3.
In AD-AS equilibrium context (vertical LRAS, equilibrium) → U3. In growth/productivity context (shifting outward) → U5.
Short-run Phillips curve (SRPC) → U3. Long-run Phillips curve (LRPC) → U5.

cross_unit_rules.situation: Government spending + central bank action in same question
cross_unit_rules.rule: If asking about COMBINATION of both → U5. If asking about one with the other as context → U3 or U4.
If testing crowding out (reduced private investment) → U5. If testing loanable funds market shift → U4. If testing AD-AS with reduced investment → U3.
As a definition/measurement → U2. As long-run equilibrium (LRPC) → U5.

common_pitfalls: Do NOT classify by keyword frequency ('taxes' 5x = U3 is wrong if the question is about money supply)
Do NOT classify by first keyword mentioned
Do NOT let distractors (wrong options) determine the unit
Do NOT ignore context: the same concept (e.g., interest rates) can be U3 or U4 depending on context
ALWAYS check: would a student who only studied this unit know this concept?
ALWAYS distinguish LRAS in equilibrium (U3) from LRAS in growth (U5)
ALWAYS distinguish fiscal policy alone (U3) from fiscal-monetary combination (U5)

unit_classification_authority.official_framework: AP Macroeconomics Course and Exam Description
unit_classification_authority.policy: Official exam and subject framework materials are the only authority for unit classification.
unit_classification_authority.student_progression_rule: A question belongs to the earliest official unit after which a student can answer it using that unit and prior units only.
